rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees that reference component ports, and in implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings.
Insert AGREE contracts inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks following AADL component type or implementation structure.
generic_rules: Use visible component features and ports in AGREE contracts; avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in type-level annexes.
Ensure AGREE variable declarations match the actual AADL data types of ports and features.
Only include assumptions and guarantees in AGREE annexes when concrete bounds or visible relations are provided.
Do not reference AADL properties like timing or unit tokens directly in AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference valid AADL feature names and types.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must align with the actual data types of the referenced ports or variables.
Do not mix AADL property names with AGREE variable scope unless explicitly defined.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless the feature is properly represented.
Do not encode AADL timing or unit properties as AGREE expressions unless they are explicitly typed and visible.
